\subsection{The matched pair: LB-real and LB-fake}
\label{sec:deps-pair}
%% §4.2 LB-real / LB-fake exposition from the user's draft. Figure 1 below.
\TODO{\S4.2 — LB-real / LB-fake exposition (from the user's draft).
Reference \cref{fig:lbpair}.}

\begin{figure}[t]
\centering
%% ---- LB-real (left) ----
\begin{minipage}[t]{0.46\linewidth}\centering
\begin{tikzpicture}[
  >={Stealth[round]},
  ev/.style={font=\small},
  po/.style={->,thick},
  rf/.style={->,dashed,bend left=18},
  sd/.style={->,thick,red!70!black,bend right=32}]
  \node[ev] (a) at (0,2)   {$a:\ \mathrm{R}\,y$};
  \node[ev] (b) at (0,0)   {$b:\ \mathrm{W}\,x{=}r_0{+}1$};
  \node[ev] (c) at (2.4,2) {$c:\ \mathrm{R}\,x$};
  \node[ev] (d) at (2.4,0) {$d:\ \mathrm{W}\,y{=}r_1{+}1$};
  \draw[po] (a) -- (b);
  \draw[po] (c) -- (d);
  \draw[rf] (b) to node[below,font=\scriptsize] {rf} (c);
  \draw[rf] (d) to node[above,font=\scriptsize] {rf} (a);
  \draw[sd] (a) to node[left,font=\scriptsize] {sdep} (b);
  \draw[sd] (c) to node[right,font=\scriptsize] {sdep} (d);
\end{tikzpicture}
\\[2pt]{\small (a) \textsf{LB-real}: $r_0{+}1$, $r_1{+}1$ \\
data dep \emph{semantic} $\Rightarrow$ WEAKEST \textbf{forbids}}
\end{minipage}\hfill
%% ---- LB-fake (right) ----
\begin{minipage}[t]{0.46\linewidth}\centering
\begin{tikzpicture}[
  >={Stealth[round]},
  ev/.style={font=\small},
  po/.style={->,thick},
  rf/.style={->,dashed,bend left=18},
  sx/.style={->,thick,gray,bend right=32,densely dotted}]
  \node[ev] (a) at (0,2)   {$a:\ \mathrm{R}\,y$};
  \node[ev] (b) at (0,0)   {$b:\ \mathrm{W}\,x{=}r_0{\oplus}r_0{\oplus}1$};
  \node[ev] (c) at (2.4,2) {$c:\ \mathrm{R}\,x$};
  \node[ev] (d) at (2.4,0) {$d:\ \mathrm{W}\,y{=}r_1{\oplus}r_1{\oplus}1$};
  \draw[po] (a) -- (b);
  \draw[po] (c) -- (d);
  \draw[rf] (b) to node[below,font=\scriptsize] {rf} (c);
  \draw[rf] (d) to node[above,font=\scriptsize] {rf} (a);
  \draw[sx] (a) to node[left,font=\scriptsize] {dep ($\notin$ sdep)} (b);
  \draw[sx] (c) to node[right,font=\scriptsize] {dep ($\notin$ sdep)} (d);
\end{tikzpicture}
\\[2pt]{\small (b) \textsf{LB-fake}: $r{\oplus}r{\oplus}1$ \\
dep \emph{syntactic only} $\Rightarrow$ WEAKEST \textbf{allows}}
\end{minipage}
\caption{The matched pair (\cref{sec:deps-pair}). Both candidate
executions wire the identical $\mathit{po}\cup\mathit{rf}$ shape and the
same observed values $r_0{=}r_1{=}1$. They differ only in whether the
read$\to$write data dependency is \emph{semantic}: in \textsf{LB-real}
the written value depends on the value read, so the edge enters
$\mathit{sdep}$ and $\mathit{jfCoherence}$ closes the cycle (forbidden);
in \textsf{LB-fake} the value $r\oplus r\oplus 1$ is constant in $r$, so
the dependency is syntactic only, excluded from $\mathit{sdep}$, and the
cycle is admitted (allowed).}
\label{fig:lbpair}
\end{figure}

\begin{figure}[t]
\centering
\begin{tikzpicture}[
  >={Stealth[round]},
  m/.style={draw,rounded corners,inner sep=3pt,font=\small},
  dom/.style={->,thick},
  ref/.style={dotted,thick,gray}]
  \node[m] (sc) at (0,2.2) {SC};
  \node[m] (tso) at (-3,0) {TSO};
  \node[m] (pso) at (-1,0) {PSO};
  \node[m] (ra)  at (1,0)  {RA};
  \node[m] (wk)  at (3.2,0) {WEAKEST};
  % SC dominates each weak model (by construction)
  \draw[dom] (sc) -- (tso);
  \draw[dom] (sc) -- (pso);
  \draw[dom] (sc) -- (ra);
  \draw[dom] (sc) -- (wk);
  % Refuted intermediate edges (Props 4.4, 4.5): dotted with a cross.
  \draw[ref] (tso) -- node[pos=0.5] {$\times$}
             node[below=2pt,font=\scriptsize,black] {Prop.~\ref{prop:tsopso}} (pso);
  \draw[ref] (ra) -- node[pos=0.5] {$\times$}
             node[below=2pt,font=\scriptsize,black] {Prop.~\ref{prop:rawk}} (wk);
\end{tikzpicture}
\caption{The encoded model lattice (\cref{sec:deps-hierarchy}). Solid
arrows are by-construction domination (\cref{thm:scdom}). SC sits above a
layer of four \emph{mutually incomparable} weak models; the dotted
crossed edges mark the two refuted intermediate containments
(\cref{prop:tsopso,prop:rawk}). There are no arrows between weak models.}
\label{fig:lattice}
\end{figure}

\caption{$\mathit{sdep}$ ablation on the WEAKEST checker
(\cref{sec:eval-ablation}). Each cell is
(\#ALLOWED, \#FORBIDDEN, \#TIMEOUT, \#ERROR) and cell wall-clock. The

every fake-dependency LB and forbids every real-dependency LB.}

\toprule
Config & LB-fake (4) & LB-real (4) & 3-thread (3) & corpus-slice (200) \\
\midrule

\textbf{all-deps-semantic} & (1,3,0,0) 64\,ms        & (0,4,0,0) 63\,ms & (3,0,0,0) 50\,ms & (194,6,0,0) 3207\,ms \\
\textbf{current}           & (4,0,0,0) 66\,ms        & (0,4,0,0) 59\,ms & (3,0,0,0) 33\,ms & (194,6,0,0) 3003\,ms \\
\bottomrule
\end{tabular}
\\[2pt]{\footnotesize $^{*}$ Absorbs JVM + first Z3-context warm-up; max
single solve across the run was 49\,ms.}
\end{table}

\label{sec:discussion}

%% wording; three-mechanisms-agree softened to literature-based).

%% =====================================================================
\section{Conclusion}
\label{sec:conclusion}
%% Fresh two-paragraph conclusion (drafted, NOT locked — clearly marked).
\textcolor{blue}{\small[DRAFTED, not locked — \cref{sec:conclusion}. Edit
freely; does not recap empirical results, per the brief.]}\par\smallskip
We have argued that out-of-thin-air behaviour can be ruled out on a single
candidate execution, without an event-structure construction, by
distinguishing \emph{semantic} from \emph{syntactic} dependencies. The
matched pair \textsf{LB-real}/\textsf{LB-fake}
(\cref{sec:deps-pair,fig:lbpair}) isolates exactly this distinction: two
executions with an identical $\mathit{po}\cup\mathit{rf}$ shape and
identical observed values that differ only in whether the read-to-write
dependency carries information. Stratifying syntactic dependencies into
the semantic sub-relation $\mathit{sdep}$, and forbidding cycles in
$\mathit{sdep}\cup\mathit{jf}$ through the $\mathit{jfCoherence}$ axiom,
yields a no-thin-air condition that is SMT-encodable and decidable on a
per-execution basis. We have implemented this condition and exercised it
on 2{,}998 supported-syntax litmus tests.

We are deliberately narrow about what this establishes. We claim no formal
correspondence with Weakestmo or with the Promising semantics: our
condition is motivated by the same grounding intuition but is not proved
equivalent to either, and we give no mechanized proof of soundness. Our
results hold for a word-sized, supported-syntax fragment, and the
dependency classifier is a finite pattern set rather than a complete
analysis. The natural next steps follow from these limits: a mechanized
proof relating $\mathit{jfCoherence}$ to an event-structure semantics; a
loader that enumerates candidate executions rather than wiring a single
one; and a fuller front-end that admits the architectural dependency and
control-dependency tests the current parser skips.
